Final Project: Academy Award Nominated Films, Violence, and the Bechdel Test

2026-05-05

Esther Lim, Ethan Sanders, Kamilia Kwiatkowska

Research Question

Among the Oscar Academy Award Best Picture shortlisted films from 2020 to 2026, is there a significant relationship between a film’s IMDb Violence and Gore rating and the number of Bechdel Test criteria it meets?

Hypotheses

Hypothesis 1 (H1)

There is a statistically detectable relationship between a film’s IMDb Violence and Gore rating and the number of Bechdel Test criteria it meets among Oscar Best Picture shortlisted films (2020-2026).

Hypothesis 2 (H2)

There is a relationship between a film being female-directed and non-female-directed and its odds of passing all three Bechdel Test criteria among Oscar Best Picture shortlisted films (2020-2026).

Hypothesis 3 (H3)

There is a relationship between a film’s worldwide box office revenue and the IMDb Violence and Gore rating among Oscar Best Picture shortlisted films (2020-2026).

Findings

Hypothesis 1

There is a statistically detectable relationship between a film’s IMDb Violence and Gore rating and the number of Bechdel Test criteria it meets among Oscar Best Picture shortlisted films (2020-2026).

Median and Mean (H1)

## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   violence     n   Mdn     M    SD
##   <ord>    <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 None        11     3  2.82 0.603
## 2 Mild        24     2  2.04 0.999
## 3 Moderate    18     3  2.5  0.857
## 4 Severe      14     3  2.14 1.23

Scatter H1

Violin H1

Boxplot H1

Spearman Test H1

## Warning in cor.test.default(x = mf[[1L]], y = mf[[2L]], ...): Cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  bechdel and violence_coded
## S = 53861, p-value = 0.5478
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##         rho 
## -0.07473071

Findings H1

There is no real correlation between Bechdel Test criteria passed and violence scores for Academy Award Best Picture nominees, because p = 0.548. PLEASE CHECK.

Hypothesis 2

H2 Of Best Picture nominees, winners of the Academy Award for Best Picture are more likely to pass Bechdel Test criteria.

Median and Mean H2

## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   result      n   Mdn     M    SD
##   <ord>   <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Winner      7     3  2.86 0.378
## 2 Nominee    60     3  2.25 1.02

Scatter H2

Violin H2

Boxplot H2

Spearman Test H2

## Warning in cor.test.default(x = mf[[1L]], y = mf[[2L]], ...): Cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  bechdel and result_coded
## S = 59275, p-value = 0.1388
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##        rho 
## -0.1827487

Findings H2

There is no real correlation between Bechdel Test criteria passed and winning the Academy Award Best Picture among Academy Award Best Picture nominees, because p = 0.139. PLEASE CHECK.

Hypothesis 3

H3 Of Best Picture nominees, winners of the Academy Award for Best Picture are more likely to score a higher level of violence.

Median and Mean H3

## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   result      n   Mdn     M    SD
##   <ord>   <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Winner      7     2  2.29 1.11 
## 2 Nominee    60     2  2.55 0.999

Scatter H3

Violin H3

## Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped.
## ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes.

Boxplot H3

Spearman Test H3

## Warning in cor.test.default(x = mf[[1L]], y = mf[[2L]], ...): Cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  violence_coded and result_coded
## S = 46230, p-value = 0.5328
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##        rho 
## 0.07754618

Findings H3

There is no real correlation between violence score and winning the Academy Award Best Picture among Academy Award Best Picture nominees, because p = 0.533. PLEASE CHECK.

Hypothesis 4

H4 The higher the Box Office revenue for an Academy Award for Best Picture nominated film, the more Bechdel Test criteria the film will pass.

Median and Mean H4

Spearman Test H4

## Warning in cor.test.default(x = mf[[1L]], y = mf[[2L]], ...): Cannot compute
## exact p-value with ties
## 
##  Spearman's rank correlation rho
## 
## data:  bechdel and box_office
## S = 36705, p-value = 0.3529
## alternative hypothesis: true rho is not equal to 0
## sample estimates:
##       rho 
## 0.1190165

Findings H4

There is no real correlation between Bechdel Test criteria met and Box Office revenue for Academy Award Best Picture nominees, because p = 0.353. PLEASE CHECK.

Hypothesis 5

H5 The higher the Box Office revenue for an Academy Award for Best Picture nominated film, the film will score a higher level of violence.
